user_query:
I'm preparing a comparative lecture on demographic and economic shifts across Atlantic island communities. Please implement and run a command-line script that reads the list of islands from input/islands.yaml, downloads official population and GDP per capita data for those economies for 1980–2015 from the World Bank Data API (domain pattern: api.worldbank.org) using indicators SP.POP.TOTL (total population) and NY.GDP.PCAP.CD (GDP per capita, current US$), and builds a clean dataset.

Requirements:
1) Implement a CLI script (you may choose the language, but it must be runnable from the shell) that:
   - Loads the economies and region_group values from input/islands.yaml.
   - Fetches both indicators via the World Bank Data API for the year range 1980–2015 (inclusive).
   - Saves the raw API responses exactly as returned (unmodified) to:
     - data/raw/population.json (for SP.POP.TOTL)
     - data/raw/gdp_per_capita.json (for NY.GDP.PCAP.CD)
   - Merges the two indicators into a single tidy CSV at output/merged.csv with columns:
     country_code,country_name,region_group,year,population,gdp_per_capita_usd
   - Aggregates to output/summary_by_region.csv with columns:
     region_group,year,total_population,mean_gdp_per_capita_usd
   - Emits a machine-readable status report at output/status.json with, at minimum, for each indicator key ("SP.POP.TOTL" and "NY.GDP.PCAP.CD"): http_status (integer), ok (boolean), economies (int count successfully processed), years {start: 1980, end: 2015}.
   - Writes its stdout/stderr to output/run_log.txt so I can inspect HTTP status codes, warnings, and any retry logic.

2) The script must be robust to API errors: if the first attempt for a dataset returns a non-200 status, retry once; record both the status code and whether the final attempt succeeded in output/status.json and ensure the error message appears in output/run_log.txt.

3) Do not hardcode data values; use only the official World Bank Data API responses. Keep the year filter and economy list configurable from the command line or config.

Deliverables to create under the current workspace:
- data/raw/population.json
- data/raw/gdp_per_capita.json
- output/merged.csv
- output/summary_by_region.csv
- output/status.json
- output/run_log.txt

I will verify by checking that the merged and summary CSVs align with the economies in input/islands.yaml, that the status JSON reflects the downloads with HTTP status codes, and that the run_log shows the command-line execution and any error messages. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
